source.title: Frequency of gamma oscillations routes flow of information in the hippocampus.

confidence_rationale: Canonical empirical demonstration that CA1 gamma fractionates into slow (~25-50Hz, CA3-coupled, memory) and fast (~60-100Hz, MEC-coupled, current position) bands locked to distinct theta phases. That theta-locked content routing is the strongest substrate evidence for MECH-294. Capped at 0.82 because the result is across-cycle segregation, not within-cycle joint binding, and the streams are homogeneous spatial/memory content -- not the four heterogeneous streams MECH-294 needs.
failure_signatures: The paper's headline finding is across-cycle segregation: slow and fast gamma occur 'mostly on different theta cycles'. That pattern is closer to a serial-multiplexing architecture than to MECH-294's within-cycle joint-binding architecture.

The two demonstrated streams are both spatial/memory and both sourced from hippocampal-system upstream. MECH-294's four streams are heterogeneous in content type and sourced from non-hippocampal regions; the gamma-routing scheme may not generalise.
The paper does not address whether downstream consumers read multiple content streams jointly within one phase-aligned window, which is the consumer-side architecture MECH-294 needs.

mapping.source_claim_statement: CA1 gamma splits into fast (60-100Hz, MEC-coherent, current-position) and slow (25-50Hz, CA3-coherent, memory-recall) bands occupying different theta phases and predominantly different theta cycles -- a temporal-segregation mechanism for content streams.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-294 inherits the gamma-routing scheme as substrate. REE upgrades it from two streams to four (goal_latent, action_proposal, risk_estimate, state_summary) and from across-cycle segregation to within-cycle joint binding.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper's central result (segregation across cycles) is closer to serial multiplexing than to joint binding. The two streams are homogeneous spatial/memory content; MECH-294 needs heterogeneous-content generalisation that this paper does not test.
mapping.source_context: Direct LFP + units in CA1, CA3, MEC of freely-behaving rats. Nature, 2009. Cited >2500 times. Anchor for the slow/fast gamma routing literature.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (Nature, replicated). Mapping fidelity moderate because the across-cycle segregation result pulls against MECH-294's within-cycle joint binding upgrade. Transfer risk moderate because demonstrated streams are homogeneous spatial/memory.
